courses.name: Introduction to Optimization (Non-Specialist Level)
topics: Linear programming: formulation, graphical solution, simplex method
Duality: dual problem formulation, weak and strong duality theorems
Sensitivity analysis: shadow prices, range analysis
Integer programming: branch and bound, cutting planes
Network flows: shortest paths, maximum flow, minimum cut
Applications in business, engineering, and operations research

courses.name: Introduction to Optimization
topics: Linear programming: convex polyhedra, basic feasible solutions, simplex method
Duality theory: dual simplex, complementary slackness, Farkas' lemma
Sensitivity analysis: parametric programming, shadow prices
Network flow theory: max-flow min-cut theorem, transportation problem
Integer linear programming: total unimodularity, cutting plane methods
Computational complexity of optimization problems

courses.name: Deterministic OR Models (Non-Specialist Level)
topics: Linear programming review: simplex method, duality
Network models: shortest paths, minimum spanning tree, maximum flow
Integer programming: modeling with binary variables, branch and bound
Dynamic programming: deterministic DP, Bellman's principle
Inventory models: EOQ, newsvendor problem
Project scheduling: CPM, PERT

courses.name: Introduction to Graph Theory
topics: Graph fundamentals: vertices, edges, degrees, isomorphism
Trees: properties, spanning trees, Cayley's formula
Connectivity: vertex connectivity, edge connectivity, Menger's theorem
Eulerian and Hamiltonian graphs: Euler tours, Hamiltonian cycles, Dirac's theorem
Graph coloring: vertex coloring, edge coloring, chromatic number
Planar graphs: Euler's formula, Kuratowski's theorem, planar duality

courses.name: Network Flow Theory
topics: Maximum flow: Ford-Fulkerson algorithm, Edmonds-Karp algorithm
Minimum cut: max-flow min-cut theorem, global minimum cut
Circulations: feasible circulations, lower bounds on flows
Minimum cost flow: successive shortest path algorithm, cycle canceling
Applications: bipartite matching, assignment problem, transportation problem
Multicommodity flows: problem formulation, complexity

courses.name: Mathematical Programming
topics: Convex analysis: convex sets, convex functions, separation theorems
Linear programming revisited: polyhedral theory, extreme points
Nonlinear programming: optimality conditions, KKT conditions
Lagrangian duality: Lagrange multipliers, dual problem
Quadratic programming: convex QP, Wolfe's method
Semidefinite programming: SDP formulation, applications

courses.name: Computational Discrete Optimization
topics: Branch and bound: search strategies, bounding techniques
Cutting plane methods: Gomory cuts, lift-and-project
Column generation: Dantzig-Wolfe decomposition
Lagrangian relaxation: subgradient optimization
Metaheuristics: simulated annealing, tabu search, genetic algorithms
Implementation using optimization solvers (CPLEX, Gurobi)

courses.name: Deterministic Optimization Models
topics: Linear programming modeling: real-world problem formulation
Integer programming modeling: binary variables, logical constraints
Network optimization: facility location, vehicle routing
Robust optimization: uncertainty modeling
Multi-objective optimization: Pareto optimality, goal programming
Case studies from logistics, finance, and telecommunications

courses.name: Portfolio Optimization Models
topics: Mean-variance optimization: Markowitz model, efficient frontier
Risk measures: VaR, CVaR, downside risk
Constraints: cardinality constraints, sector constraints, transaction costs
Black-Litterman model: incorporating investor views
Robust portfolio optimization: uncertainty in returns
Implementation using quadratic programming

courses.name: Algebraic Enumeration
topics: Generating functions: ordinary, exponential, rational
Pólya's enumeration theorem: group actions, cycle index
Species theory: combinatorial species, operations on species
Asymptotic enumeration: singularity analysis, saddle point method
q-analogues: Gaussian binomial coefficients, q-series
Applications to graph enumeration and permutation statistics

courses.name: Graph Theory
topics: Matchings: Hall's marriage theorem, Tutte's theorem, Edmonds' algorithm
Graph connectivity: Menger's theorem, network reliability
Graph minors: Wagner's theorem, Robertson-Seymour theory
Extremal graph theory: Turán's theorem, Ramsey theory
Spectral graph theory: adjacency matrix, Laplacian eigenvalues
Algebraic graph theory: automorphism groups, Cayley graphs

courses.name: Integer Programming
topics: Polyhedral theory: convex hull of integer points, lattice points
Cutting planes: Chvátal-Gomory cuts, split cuts
Branch and cut: integration of cutting planes with branch and bound
Total unimodularity: network matrices, applications
Integer programming duality: superadditive dual functions
Advanced methods: branch and price, branch and cut and price

courses.name: Large-Scale Optimization
topics: Decomposition methods: Dantzig-Wolfe decomposition, Benders decomposition
Column generation: pricing problems, stabilization techniques
Lagrangian relaxation: dual ascent, bundle methods
Interior point methods: barrier functions, primal-dual methods
Parallel and distributed optimization: ADMM
Applications to large-scale linear and integer programs

courses.name: Introduction to Game Theory
topics: Normal form games: Nash equilibrium, best response dynamics
Zero-sum games: minimax theorem, linear programming formulation
Extensive form games: subgame perfect equilibrium, backward induction
Cooperative games: core, Shapley value, bargaining solutions
Evolutionary game theory: replicator dynamics, evolutionary stable strategies
Auction theory: revenue equivalence, winner's curse

courses.name: Convex Optimization and Analysis
topics: Convex analysis: convex sets, convex functions, subgradients
Convex optimization: optimality conditions, duality theory
Gradient methods: steepest descent, conjugate gradient
Proximal algorithms: proximal gradient, ADMM
Second-order methods: Newton's method, quasi-Newton methods
Applications in machine learning and signal processing

courses.name: Continuous Optimization
topics: Unconstrained optimization: line search, trust region methods
Constrained optimization: penalty methods, augmented Lagrangian
Sequential quadratic programming (SQP)
Interior point methods for nonlinear programming
Derivative-free optimization: Nelder-Mead, pattern search
Global optimization: branch and bound, Lipschitz optimization

courses.name: History of Combinatorics and Optimization
topics: Historical development of combinatorial mathematics
Origins of graph theory: Euler, Hamilton, Kirkman
Development of linear programming: Kantorovich, Dantzig, von Neumann
The simplex method and its impact
Integer programming and combinatorial optimization milestones
Key figures: Edmonds, Gomory, Cook, Karp

courses.name: Introduction to Mathematical Cryptography
topics: Classical ciphers: substitution, transposition, Vigenère
Symmetric key cryptography: AES, DES, modes of operation
Public key cryptography: RSA, ElGamal, elliptic curve cryptography
Cryptographic hash functions: SHA-2, SHA-3, applications
Digital signatures: RSA signatures, DSA, ECDSA
Cryptographic protocols: key exchange, authentication, zero-knowledge proofs

courses.name: Applied Cryptography
topics: Number theory for cryptography: primality testing, discrete logarithms
RSA and its variants: padding schemes, attacks
Elliptic curve cryptography: ECDH, ECDSA
Post-quantum cryptography: lattice-based, code-based, multivariate cryptography
Protocol security: TLS, SSH, IPsec
Implementation issues: side-channel attacks, timing attacks, fault injection

courses.name: Combinatorial Quantum Field Theory
topics: Analytic combinatorics techniques for graph enumeration
Feynman diagrams and combinatorial structures in QFT
Perturbative expansions and their combinatorial aspects
Lorentzian polynomials and generalized permutahedra
Connections between algebraic geometry, topology, and statistical models
Applications to quantum field theory and particle physics
